exampleSentencesTranslated: I broke the cup you just bought by accident yesterday.
I was late to the meeting this morning by accident because I overslept.
Sorry, I forgot to bring the important documents by accident.
notes:
lỡ = by accident, unintentionally. It’s used when something bad or inconvenient happened and you didn’t mean it.

It usually comes before a verb: lỡ + V (lỡ làm, lỡ quên, lỡ nói). It often carries a slight feeling of regret.

It can also mean “too late” or “missed the chance” depending on context (lỡ rồi = it’s already too late).

Close words: vô tình (unintentionally, more neutral), trót (did something wrong and regret it — stronger guilt).

definitions.vi: Bỏ lỡ hoặc không bắt kịp cái gì đó.
definitions.en: To miss or to fail to catch something.

example.vi: Tôi đã lỡ chuyến xe buýt.

exampleSentences: Em lỡ làm vỡ chiếc cốc mẹ mới mua hôm qua.
Anh lỡ ngủ quên nên đến muộn buổi họp sáng nay.
Chị xin lỗi vì lỡ quên mang theo tài liệu quan trọng.
notes_md:
**lỡ** = by accident, unintentionally. It’s used when something bad or inconvenient happened and you didn’t mean it.

It usually comes before a verb: *lỡ + V* (lỡ làm, lỡ quên, lỡ nói). It often carries a slight feeling of regret.

It can also mean “too late” or “missed the chance” depending on context (lỡ rồi = it’s already too late).

Close words: **vô tình** (unintentionally, more neutral), **trót** (did something wrong and regret it — stronger guilt).
